title: podman machine init for HyperV provider fails on non English Windows
body:
### Issue Description

When running podman machine init with HyperV on my French Windows, I have this:
```

Downloading VM image: fedora-coreos-38.20231027.2.0-hyperv.x86_64.vhdx.zip: done
Extracting compressed file: test_fedora-coreos-38.20231027.2.0-hyperv.x86_64.vhdx: done
Error: could not find virtual machine "test"
```

Although the HyperV machine has been created:

![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/695993/5e72a503-3fdb-4986-a154-ba955fe1f90b)


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. set CONTAINERS_MACHINE_PROVIDER=hyperv
2. podman machine init
3.


### Describe the results you received

Error returned while the machine has been created

### Describe the results you expected

Machine should be created

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Seems to be related to the underlying libhvee (I18N)

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: @baude @mheon @ashley-cui PTAL
you are, for certain, running this with from a shell with administrative privileges?
also, please provide the full invocation ?

> you are, for certain, running this with from a shell with administrative privileges?

I have not but then switched to an admin shell and got the same error

> > you are, for certain, running this with from a shell with administrative privileges?
> 
> I have not but then switched to an admin shell and got the same error



> also, please provide the full invocation ?

podman machine init testhyperv

I switched my windows language to French and wasn't able to reproduce this.

Can you see if translating `Microsoft Virtual Machine` to 
`Machine virtuelle Microsoft` (or something like that) here fixes anything for you? 

https://github.com/containers/libhvee/blob/ec235380978ccf81dd6af42696c92a16bc5fe61f/pkg/hypervctl/vmm.go#L27

> I switched my windows language to French and wasn't able to reproduce this.
> 
> Can you see if translating `Microsoft Virtual Machine` to `Machine virtuelle Microsoft` (or something like that) here fixes anything for you?
> 
> https://github.com/containers/libhvee/blob/ec235380978ccf81dd6af42696c92a16bc5fe61f/pkg/hypervctl/vmm.go#L27

Changing the WQL query from:
`Select * From Msvm_ComputerSystem Where Description = 'Microsoft Virtual Machine'`
to
`Select * From Msvm_ComputerSystem Where Description = 'Ordinateur virtuel Microsoft'`

causes the dumpvms tools to return the list of Hyper-V VMs instead of `null`
Oh this might be interesting. @baude @n1hility it looks like the query changes depending on the system install language. Any ideas on how to fix this? Is there a hyperV config for language?
Just a thought - I wonder if it has to do with having the english locale existing on the system too - I was unable to reproduce on my system which has english installed.

@ashley-cui yeah thats exactly what i was thinking it might be that it would ignore and fallback to the system default without an error. I was able to get it to happen but i had to remove all traces of the english language pack, and then reboot (otherwise it gets cached)

@jeffmaury could you going into windows settings, languages and adding the english language pack and doing a full OS reboot?. You shouldn't have to switch your display language or anything like that, Get-InstalledLanguage should show an en language pack afterwords and it should start working.

Thanks for reproducing! I suppose the thing to do here is document this behavior, as I guess the fix is to download the language pack in Windows settings. 

cc @baude

yeah we could detect it isnt available and throw a friendlier error with instructions. alternatively it might be possible to do some specialized queries that better adapt to arbitrary locales in the future (e.g. via indirect querying, or possibly some synced locale keyword table). Before going with the latter it needs some research to verify we dont end up with something too brittle to maintain.

> @ashley-cui yeah thats exactly what i was thinking it might be that it would ignore and fallback to the system default without an error. I was able to get it to happen but i had to remove all traces of the english language pack, and then reboot (otherwise it gets cached)
> 
> @jeffmaury could you going into windows settings, languages and adding the english language pack and doing a full OS reboot?. You shouldn't have to switch your display language or anything like that, Get-InstalledLanguage should show an en language pack afterwords and it should start working.

Once I added the English US language pack, then dumpvms now works ok

> yeah we could detect it isnt available and throw a friendlier error with instructions. alternatively it might be possible to do some specialized queries that better adapt to arbitrary locales in the future (e.g. via indirect querying, or possibly some synced locale keyword table). Before going with the latter it needs some research to verify we dont end up with something too brittle to maintain.

I'm afraid that will be too complex for users to handle and not sure it would be supported in enterprise environments. Does the Hyper-V Host Compute System API has the same limitations ?

> > @ashley-cui yeah thats exactly what i was thinking it might be that it would ignore and fallback to the system default without an error. I was able to get it to happen but i had to remove all traces of the english language pack, and then reboot (otherwise it gets cached)
> > @jeffmaury could you going into windows settings, languages and adding the english language pack and doing a full OS reboot?. You shouldn't have to switch your display language or anything like that, Get-InstalledLanguage should show an en language pack afterwords and it should start working.
> 
> Once I added the English US language pack, then dumpvms now works ok

Awesome. Thanks for confirming

> I'm afraid that will be too complex for users to handle 

Yeah I agree ideally we would automate it if its required but first going to try an approach I have in mind where it wouldn't matter.

> and not sure it would be supported in enterprise environments. 

It would really just be a dependent package if going this route (similar overhead would apply to distributing podman for example). Although I agree even better if it's avoided. 

> Does the Hyper-V Host Compute System API has the same limitations ?

I think it's immune to this one via the JSON schema but  it does trade other problems. Will get back soon on a possible solution once I escape some meetings :)

FYI a fix is advancing through to podman:
https://github.com/containers/libhvee/pull/62
Scary because the path works for me but the old code also (0.4.0) so it seems if you install and remove a language pack some files are kept

> Scary because the path works for me but the old code also (0.4.0) so it seems if you install and remove a language pack some files are kept

Problem explained: language pack needs to be uninstalled using Powershell Uninstall-language, removing it through control panel is not enough. Once I did it and reboot, 0.4.0 is broken and patch is ok
closed via https://github.com/containers/libhvee/pull/62
